response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering expertise and 19 years of development experience, they lack the core technical requirement: only 6 months of documented C++ experience versus the required 8–12 years. Recent career focus has been TypeScript/Node.js and web platforms rather than systems programming. Critically absent are industrial automation domain knowledge, cybersecurity standards (SDL, IEC 62443), IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus), and industrial security expertise—all core to the role. Current career gap since October 2025 adds additional concern.
strengths: Extensive software engineering breadth (19 years) with proven ability to architect high-performance distributed systems (Dunnhumby: millions of requests/second)
Demonstrated mentoring and thought leadership through open-source projects, workshops, and public talks on software craftsmanship
Strong full-stack capabilities and polyglot experience, suggesting adaptability and systems-level thinking
concerns: Critical gap: only 6 months of documented C++ experience (2014–2015) despite C++ being the core technical requirement for 8–12 years
Missing industrial automation, IoT, and cybersecurity expertise entirely—no mention of MQTT, Modbus, IEC 62443, SDL practices, or industrial protocols
Recent career trajectory (10 years) focused on TypeScript, Node.js, web, and GIS platforms; no recent systems-level or C++ work to demonstrate currency

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise and systems programming depth
Industrial automation and cybersecurity standards knowledge
Breadth of engineering experience and mentoring capability
